column1: # AutoTLM One — the locked production partition table (owner ruling 2026-07-14).

column2: TWO generous OTA app slots (1.875 MB each)

column1: # unit flashed from here on can receive BLE and OTA features later with no
# field re-partition. OTA is a green-lit feature
# ⚠️ otadata MUST live at 0xE000 and app0 at 0x10000. The esp32 core hardcodes
# those two offsets as literals in its upload / merge-bin / program recipes
# that moves them makes every arduino-cli/IDE upload write the app into
# whatever partition happens to sit at 0x10000 — the bootloader then finds no
# bootable slot and the unit boot-loops. Boards 0.3.0 shipped that bug (NVS was

column2: pushing app0 to 0x20000) and bricked the bench One on its

column1: # first flash. Keep these offsets standard; grow the app slots
